daily_load_assessment: Today's training readiness is excellent. Your CTL of 31.8 against an ATL of 37.9 places your Training Stress Balance (TSB) at a fresh -6.1, which is inside the optimal zone for high-quality stimulation. Resting HR is exceptionally stable at 49 bpm. Yesterday was a low-stress commute day, meaning there is zero residual fatigue preventing you from executing a high-performance session today.
acute_status_assessment_3_weeks: Over the past 21 days, your volume of 14.93 hours is deceptive. Out of 19 sessions, 13 were low-intensity commutes and only 4 were runs, with only 2 true quality sessions. Your current training state is heavily biased toward low-intensity aerobic base commuting. While this maintains basic health, it provides insufficient high-intensity stimulus to shift your FTP of 250W or improve your 5km running capacity.
sport_trend_assessment_6_months: Your 180-day trend reveals a stagnated CTL hovering around 31.8. This flatline is a direct result of relying on unstructured commuting miles rather than progressive performance training. To move your FTP from 3.29 W/kg to a higher bracket and drop your 5k running times, we must shift the focus of your athletic trend from volume to structured, high-intensity intervals that target your VO2max.
coach_verdict: The numbers do not lie: you are highly recovered but undertrained in the quality zones. Commute volume will not increase your FTP or run pace. Today, you are cleared and expected to execute a high-intensity session. Choose either the VO2max cycling block or the 5k running intervals. No cutting corners.

cycling_option.workout_details: 15 min Warm-up progressing from 150W to 220W. 3x 30-sec priming efforts at 300W. Main Set: 5 x 4 minutes at 285W - 300W (115-120% FTP), with 4 minutes of fully passive recovery between reps. 10 min Cool-down.
cycling_option.reason: Directly targets your goal of improving 5-minute cycling power and raising your VO2max ceiling to break through your 250W FTP plateau.

running_option.workout_details: 2 km easy warm-up jog, followed by 3x 100m strides. Main Set: 5 x 1000m at your target 5km race pace (Aim for Heart Rate Zone 4/5, reaching ~175-185 bpm by the end of each interval). Take 2 minutes of walking/jogging recovery between each interval. 1.5 km easy warm-down jog.
running_option.reason: Develops the neuromuscular efficiency, mental toughness, and lactic threshold necessary to run a highly competitive 5km.

recovery_option.workout_details: 40 minutes of flat, easy cycling strictly below 135W (under 55% of FTP). Keep cadence high (90-95 RPM). Follow with 15 minutes of lower-body dynamic stretching and foam rolling, focusing on calves, hamstrings, and hip flexors.
recovery_option.reason: Only select this if you are experiencing subjective muscle soreness or high cognitive fatigue. It will promote blood flow to accelerate recovery for a high-intensity block tomorrow.
